items.title: Labor, Greens cut deal on CGT and negative gearing

children.content: The Greens pledge their support in exchange for an end to an exemption that allows self-managed super funds to borrow money to invest in residential property, and a delay on the overhaul of the NDIS.

articles.title: Angus Taylor refuses to say Coalition backs multicultural policy

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has refused to say whether the Coalition backs multicultural policy in Australia. Follow live.

image.altText: Angus Taylor speaks to the media.

articles.title: Pauline Hanson clarifies she will not seek to cut paid parental leave

children.content: Pauline Hanson has clarified she would not seek to cut government-funded paid parental leave if One Nation were in power after opening the door to a wind-back at her National Press Club address last week.

image.altText: A woman in a red, with red hair, standing in a corridor in front of TV cameras.

articles.title: Hanson says Ben Roberts-Smith should be welcome at War Memorial event

children.content: Federal parliament will adjourn early today so politicians can attend the official opening of a hall as part of the controversial $550 million extension of the War Memorial.

image.altText: A man wearing many medals stands out from the crowd.

gallery.altText: two male politicians walking through glass double doors

thumbnail.altText: two male politicians walking through glass double doors

items.title: Australia approves restricted use of herbicide banned in 70 other countries

children.content: The Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority concludes its nearly 30-year review of the controversial but popular herbicide.

image.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

items.title: 'We lost our son': India's medical exams scandal leads to tragedy and uproar

children.content: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

image.altText: Four women in floral veils have their heads bowed, holding a photo of a young man wearing glasses.

items.title: 'They've been caught out': KPMG fallout continues with resignations

children.content: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard and two audit partners are leaving the accounting and consulting giant in the wake of a scandal over misuse of confidential client information and the alleged mistreatment of a whistleblower.

image.altText: KPMG

items.title: WiseTech executive chair Richard White denies human trafficking reports

children.content: WiseTech initially gained ground but is now in the red again after Monday's 18 per cent plunge. The ASX is seesawing between gains and losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The blurred side profile of a man walking past a screen that says "ASX welcomes WiseTech Global".

images.altText: Erling Haaland holds his arms skyward in celebration
Kylian Mbappe points towards the crowd after scoring a goal
Lionel Messi holds his hands up and smiles

articles.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

image.altText: Lionel Messi waves to the crowd as he walks off the field after a game

articles.title: Quick hits: Wild weather, Messi magic and Merlin meets the president

children.content: Some wild weather brings the World Cup to a standstill, but not before Lionel Messi lights it up to rewrite the record books. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Merlin the duck, Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe

children.content: Kylian Mbappe and France put the foot down against Iraq after inclement weather forces a two-hour delay, while Erling Haaland nets again as Norway takes on Senegal. Follow live.

items.title: Haaland, Mbappe and Messi make it another magic World Cup day

updates.title: 81' Algeria score again through Amine Gouiri

items.title: Who wins and who loses in the NSW budget

children.content: With billions for health but limited funds for the state's regions, here are the 2026 NSW budget's winners and losers.

image.altText: A graphic of the Australia map in blue with NSW bolded, and gold coins around the map.

items.title: The winners and losers in Queensland's state budget

children.content: The Queensland treasurer has handed down the state budget. Take a look at what it includes and who benefits.

image.altText: a graphic of a map of queensland with gold coins

items.title: Newly discovered spider uses 'ballista' web to capture prey

children.content: A bizarre species of spider from the rainforests of Far North Queensland springs a nasty surprise.

image.altText: Close up vision of a small spider tangling above an ant on a leaf

gallery.altText: A close up of a leaf an ant approaches a cone of spider web while a spider hovers above

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a leaf an ant approaches a cone of spider web while a spider hovers above

items.title: Nose landing gear failure could have caused fatal plane crash, ATSB says

children.content: A plane crash that killed two people and injured several others in Adelaide earlier this year could have been caused by a failure in the plane's nose landing gear, an ongoing investigation has found.

image.altText: Smoke coming from buildings behind a fence

items.title: Photo gives clues to missing man's final hours before disappearance

children.content: A previously unreleased image answers one question in the case of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan, but many others remain.

image.altText: Jack McLennan jumps into Barambah Creek hours before he vanished.

items.title: Meet the 'King of the North' set to become the UK's new PM

children.content: A popular mayor but a virtual unknown on the global stage, Andy Burnham is poised to become Sir Keir Starmer's successor. Here's what that could look like.

articles.title: 'They've lost the plot': UK Labour could learn from Rudd-Gillard soap opera

children.content: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

image.altText: Ken Rudd, wearing a dark suit, gestures with his right hand while holding a pen. Julia Gillard is in the background.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

articles.title: More or Less quiz: Which British PMs have lasted the longest?

children.content: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

image.altText: A screenshot from a Daily Star broadcast shows a lettuce with googly eyes next to portrait of Liz Truss

articles.title: Andy Burnham sworn in as MP in hours after Starmer's resignation

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer says he will resign as prime minister of the United Kingdom and spend more time as a husband and father.

image.altText: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer hugs his wife Victoria

items.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie is dead, US media reports

children.content: CNN reported that one of the notes claimed that the mother of NBC's Today show host, Savannah Guthrie, was dead, and those who kidnapped her did not mean to kill her, but she died shortly after her disappearance.

image.altText: A photo of Savannah Guthrie alongside her mother Nancy behind a crowd with posters.

items.title: Award-winning artwork revealed as 'blatant copy'

children.content: The Doyles award committee is investigating why it took nearly 12 months to discover a winning landscape painting was an imitation of another artist's work.

image.altText: A photo of an artwork depicting two childlike figures at the beach.

items.title: 'Like looking into a mirror': I met my siblings at the age of 35

children.content: Cheryl van Wageningen was shocked to receive a letter revealing that not only was she Indigenous, but she had a brother and a sister.

gallery.altText: An old photo of two women sitting next to each other on a brick wall wearing dresses

thumbnail.altText: An old photo of two women sitting next to each other on a brick wall wearing dresses

items.title: A 'baby' Lila McGuire dreamt of a Logie nomination. Yesterday it came true

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

image.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

items.title: You won't see Hugh Jackman the same after The Death of Robin Hood

children.content: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

image.altText: Hugh Jackman in The Death of Robin Hood

items.title: Perfect dupes of ABC articles are fuelling an industrial-scale scam

children.content: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

gallery.altText: An illustration of a phone inside a bear trap

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of a phone inside a bear trap

items.title: Qatar gas complex explosion leaves at least 13 people dead

children.content: The Sunday night blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility is one of the deadliest events recorded in gas industry.

image.altText: The sunsets over power lines and pylons at the Ras Laffan industrial city

items.title: Security agencies issue blunt 'act swiftly' warning on AI

children.content: The Five Eyes agencies warn leaders must "act swiftly" to address the imminent threat as AI increases the "speed, scale and sophistication of cyber threats".

image.altText: A row of men in suits sitting on a stage

items.title: Australians have record-low trust in the United States, new poll finds

children.content: The Lowy Institute's latest poll shows Australia's trust in the US has fallen to a record low.

image.altText: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump walk together along the Temple of Heaven.

items.title: Police urge residents to search backyards for missing Victorian man

children.content: Police are urging residents to check backyards, sheds and outdoor settings for missing Victorian man Neville Nicholson who has been missing since last Wednesday.

image.altText: A video frame from CCTV footage of the last known sighting of missing a Horsham man

items.title: High cost of living fuels record regional migration, report says

children.content: The rate at which capital city dwellers are moving the regions is at its highest level on record, which the report's author says shows the trend is more than a post-COVID blip.

gallery.altText: Aerial view of sailing boats on a water with a city as a backdrop

thumbnail.altText: Aerial view of sailing boats on a water with a city as a backdrop

avatar.alt: A profile image of a woman with dark hair.

items.title: Victorian farm fined for not meeting free-range standards

children.content: Doreen Egg admits it sold eggs labelled as "free range" despite its hens not having access to an outdoor range, as required under Free Range Egg Labelling Information Standard.

image.altText: A cluster of hens crowded towards the camera, blue sky and grass behind.

items.title: Union launches bid to save Boag's brewery from closure

children.content: The United Workers Union has launched a petition calling on Boag's parent company Lion Australia to sell rather than close its Launceston brewery. The union has also written to Premier Jeremy Rockliff urging him to intervene.

image.altText: red brick brewery with boags signage and a big green beer can

items.title: Inspired by volcanoes, this new technology could affect billions of people

children.content: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains a billion people depend on.

gallery.altText: A plane flies around the earth, dropping particles into the sky

thumbnail.altText: A plane flies around the earth, dropping particles into the sky

items.title: Potent sedative found in vapes sold in Melbourne

children.content: Victorian health authorities say vape products containing the powerful sedative etomidate have been found being sold illegally.

image.altText: An illegal vape.

items.title: Music mogul Clive Davis dies at 94 leaving behind powerful imprint on industry

children.content: Clive Davis, the influential music industry mogul who fostered the careers of superstars such as Whitney Houston, Bruce Springsteen and Alicia Keys, has died. He was 94.

image.altText: Clive Davis in a shiny suit holding up a peace sign, wearing tinted glasses

items.title: Egg shortage unlikely as poultry industry braces for deadly bird flu

children.content: The poultry industry says it is concerned but prepared as the deadly H5 avian influenza variant reaches Australian shores for the first time.

gallery.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

thumbnail.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

items.title: Former US Federal Reserve chair Alan Greenspan dies aged 100

children.content: The wife of the former federal reserve chair says he died from complications of Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: Alan Greenman wearing a suit with a red tie and glasses while smiling.

items.title: Christian Brothers 'squib' on abuse compensation, lawyer says

children.content: The Catholic order says it will sell off its remaining assets to fund abuse compensation, but the money raised is expected to fall short of what is owed to victims.

image.altText: Cross on top of a church

items.title: Government responses criticised two months on from Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Eight weeks on from the alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby, Alice Springs community leaders are calling for an audit to determine why federal and territory government spending does not seem to be improving Indigenous living conditions and safety.

gallery.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

thumbnail.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

avatar.alt: Jane Bardon in a blazer looking at the camera.

items.title: Logging company gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria

children.content: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

image.altText: A semi-trailer loaded with logs in a traffic lane approaching a port. The large Spirit of Tasmania ship is in front of it.

items.title: Ex-Wimbledon champ handed four-year suspension for refusing doping test

children.content: Markéta Vondroušová, who lifted the Wimbledon trophy in 2023, is given a "significant ban" by the International Tennis Integrity Agency.

image.altText: Marketa Vondrousova holds the Wimbledon trophy in 2023.

items.title: Australian sprinter feels 'absolutely robbed' after Commonwealth Games snub

children.content: Josh Azzopardi opens up after missing out on selection as a 100 metres runner in Australia's athletics team for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

gallery.altText: Josh Azzopardi stands in front of palm trees at the Oceania Athletics Championships.

thumbnail.altText: Josh Azzopardi stands in front of palm trees at the Oceania Athletics Championships.
